title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium on May 16, with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Luca Langoni and Brayan Ceballos returned to the lineup, helping the Revolution achieve a fast start against Minnesota United.
Matt Turner redeemed himself with key saves after previous criticism, reinforcing his form despite a penalty goal for Minnesota.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'SNL' Season 51 finale on May 16, featuring Paul McCartney as the musical guest.
Ferrell opened the show playing Jeffrey Epstein's ghost in a skit with James Austin Johnson's Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' from his upcoming album, during the finale.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 is set for July 11-16, featuring ships from over 25 countries for the Sail250 tour.
Sail Boston events have a rich history, with notable occurrences in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017.
Protests during the 1992 and 2000 events highlighted historical grievances linked to the ships, yet attendance remained high.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returned to Patriots rookie minicamp, improving the team's vibe despite his offseason personal scandal.
The Patriots are reportedly set to acquire wide receiver A.J. Brown before the start of the 2026 season.
Newly acquired safety Kevin Byard praised Mike Vrabel's energy and coaching style during the rookie minicamp.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a go-ahead two-run home run in the eighth inning, lifting the Red Sox over the Braves 3-2.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two runs on four hits, with the Red Sox achieving a comeback win.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save despite a bases-loaded situation, helping the Red Sox even the weekend series against Atlanta.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day IL with a sports hernia, considers potential surgery with the Red Sox.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on Boston Red Sox roster.
Story batting .206, has committed six errors in 39 games, struggled with injuries since joining in 2022.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old pickup driver died in a head-on collision with a school bus in Marlborough, NH, on Route 12.
The school bus, driven by James French, 77, carried 13 students; none sustained serious injuries, police confirmed.
The crash investigation is ongoing by NH State Police; witnesses are encouraged to contact authorities with information.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th gave the Braves a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs, with six strikeouts in five innings.
The Red Sox managed seven hits, leaving seven men on base, continuing struggles in scoring runs.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots' reunion with Stefon Diggs unlikely due to pending trade for A.J. Brown, according to Albert Breer.
Stefon Diggs expressed interest in re-signing but Patriots' budget focused on A.J. Brown and Romeo Doubs.
Bright offensive outlook remains for Patriots with expected trade improvements, despite uncertainty over Diggs.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as Black, 5'8" tall, thin build, with brown eyes, and black hair; frequents Columbia Road and Harambee Park.
Public urged to contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 with information; anonymous tips accepted through Boston Police CrimeStoppers.
